ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-07-2026 Continued To Jun-09-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas Wrobel for administrator Edward Calvin Lawson III (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Order to Show Cause Why Administrator Should Not Be Removed and Sanctioned Further for Failure to File Required Documents and Failure to Appear.

The Court noted that administrator Edward Lawson did not appear. Mr. Wrobel reported that he had not had contact with Mr. Lawson despite attempts to reach him and to advise him of the consequences of nonappearance. The Court confirmed that the Order to Show Cause and prior order imposing sanctions had been served on Mr. Lawson.

The Court imposed further sanctions in the amount of $250 against Mr. Lawson. The Court suspended Mr. Lawson as administrator and appointed the Public Administrator as temporary administrator of the estate.

The Court continued the matter to June 9, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, and stated that an Order to Appear/Order to Show Cause shall issue Mr. Lawson to explain why he should not be removed as administrator and why additional sanctions in the amount of $250 should not be imposed.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-03-2026 Continued To Apr-07-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Wrobel appeared via zoom for Edward Calvin Lawson III(petitioner).

Mr. Wrobel stated that he has been unsuccessful in reaching his client, and that he is the sole beneficiary of the estate.

The Court issues sanctions in the amount of $250.00 against Edward Calvin Lawson III Pursuant to CCP 177.5 payable within 10 days of the order.

An Order to Appear and Order to Show Cause will be issued to Edward Calvin Lawson III (petitioner) to appear on April 7, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to show cause as to why administrator should not be removed and further sanctions should not be assessed for failure to appear and comply with court orders. Order to show cause is also continued to the same date.

Petition for final distribution is ordered off calendar

Judge: Russell S. Roeca. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Obitinalla. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Feb-03-2026 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Nov-18-2025 Continued To Feb-03-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas Wrobel for petitioner Edward Calvin Lawson III (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Petition for Final Distribution.

The Court advised that, despite counsel's efforts, written confirmation from the Department of Health Care Services (DHCS) regarding the voluntary lien was still required before the petition could be granted. Mr. Wrobel acknowledged the issue and explained the difficulty in obtaining notarized documentation from his client, noting that communication delays had prolonged the process. He suggested that an Order to Show Cause might assist in compelling his client to complete the required notarization.

The Court agreed to issue an Order to Show Cause directing the petitioner to appear and show cause why sanctions should not be imposed for failure to provide the required DHCS documentation. The Court noted that if the necessary documents are filed prior to the next hearing, the OSC will be taken off calendar. The hearing on the Order to Show Cause shall be set for February 3, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The petition for final distribution was continued to the same date.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Appearances:
Thomas Wrobel for Edward Lawson III (petitioner) (via zoom).

Mr. Wrobel stated that his client signed a voluntary lien, that they had issues with the notarization agreement with the Department of Health Care, and that should clear the creditors claim. The matter is continued to October 7, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Not reported.
